interpretation: CPPED1 encodes a serine/threonine protein phosphatase that specifically dephosphorylates AKT at 'Ser-473', which is crucial for regulating cell cycle progression and apoptosis. In the context of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), the dysregulation of cell survival and apoptosis pathways is a known mechanism contributing to bone cell death and subsequent osteonecrosis. However, the retrieved evidence does not directly link CPPED1 to SONFH, indicating a lack of specific studies or data connecting this gene to the disease. Therefore, while the biological function of CPPED1 suggests a potential role in osteonecrosis through its effects on cell survival, the evidence is weak and primarily inferred. As such, CPPED1 may not serve as a reliable early-detection biomarker for SONFH based on current data.
relevance_summary: CPPED1 may influence pathways relevant to bone cell survival, but lacks direct evidence linking it to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head.

result.title: CPPED1 (Q9BRF8)
result.text:
Protein: Serine/threonine-protein phosphatase CPPED1
Function: Protein phosphatase that dephosphorylates AKT family kinase specifically at 'Ser-473', blocking cell cycle progression and promoting cell apoptosis. May play an inhibitory role in glucose uptake by adipocytes

Description: Abnormal placentation in which the placenta implants in the lower segment of the uterus (the zone of dilation) and may cover part or all of the opening of the cervix. It is often associated with serious antepartum bleeding and premature labor.
